steps.title: Spot the relative clause
steps.content: The relative pronoun “that” introduces a relative clause with “bits of molten rock” as the subject. An adverbial phrase—”after cooling and solidifying into glass”—interrupts the clause between the subject and the blank.

steps.title: Choose the finite verb form
steps.content: “Rain” is the only finite verb form, which relative clauses require as their main verb. The other three options—”to rain,” “raining,” and “having rained”—are all non-finite verb forms that cannot serve in this role. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
